brief_justification: Arrives at the correct answer (No) with valid multi-layer causal reasoning tracing severity → organ function → positivity violation; traces second and third-order effects including trajectory encoding and sequential confound discovery as a signal of unobserved confounders; explicitly monitors own reasoning by naming where magnitudes are unknown and identifying the stopping-point problem; applies explicit falsification test listing three conditions that would need to hold to assert benefit, none verifiable; clearly distinguishes known facts (selection criteria) from unknowns (post-adjustment effect direction and magnitude); systematically evaluates all three directional outcomes (reduction, null, increase) and identifies the RCT/IV alternative; every conclusion traces to an explicit derivation—the table, the organ-function bias direction, the positivity argument; explicitly avoids treating the observational adjusted estimate as a causal identification, showing awareness that no valid identification strategy succeeds; exhibits zero suppressed failure modes—no observational causation inference, no single-hypothesis commitment, no overconfidence; reasoning path maps directly to topology (S1→classify→list confounds→check controllability→G2 not all blocked→seek instruments→identification fails→reject and restate as association).
